figures.caption: Table 2 All 20 ManipArena tasks. Execution Reasoning tasks have visually unambiguous goals—the challenge is precise motor execution. Semantic Reasoning tasks require resolving semantic ambiguity before acting. MobileManipulation tasks require long-horizon navigation and manipulation.

Figure 8 Real2Sim environment construction. The physical workspace is reconstructed via 3D Gaussian Splatting, task objects are generated with Hunyuan3D, and the simulation runs on IsaacLab with trajectory-level alignment to real-robot data.

Figure 10 (a) Basket position robustness on pick_items_into_basket: DreamZero is nearly position-invariant (−8%) while VLA models degrade −44% to −57%. (b) Category performance breakdown: OneModel’s advantage over Single concentrates on semantic tasks (+16%).

Table 1 Comparison of ManipArena with existing benchmarks. Prior works are either simulator-centric or lack standardized real-world evaluation. ManipArena uniquely provides a comprehensive, standardized framework bridging simulation and real-world deployment with reasoning-intensive tasks and multi-level generalization.
Figure 4 The training diversity of three hierarchical levels.
Table 7 56-dimensional state/action specification for tabletop tasks. All modalities are recorded at 20Hz from the follow (robot) arms.
Table 6 Evaluation generalization design per task. Tasks are categorized by whether T9–T10 introduce true OOD objects or test configuration robustness within the training distribution.
Figure 7 Data sample distributions across these same 4 units.
Table 4 Baseline evaluation on 15 tabletop tasks. Score: total out of 100 (10 trials × 10 pts). SR: success rate (≥9/trial). Best per task in bold.
Figure 1 Representative examples from each task category.

Figure 12 Model capability boundaries across five core dimensions. DreamZero dominates coarse manipulation and spatial robustness; OneModel leads on semantic understanding and precision; π0.5-Single excels at sequential reasoning.
Table 5 Full diversity specification per task. Dim = number of explicitly controlled diversity dimensions. Traj = number of training trajectories (from open-source release).
Figure 2 Three types of reasoning tasks in ManipArena.

Figure 11 OOD degradation on two representative tasks. Top: sort_headphone—VLA collapses at compound OOD (−95%/−100%) while DreamZero degrades gracefully (−33%). Bottom: put_spoon—OneModel shows severe degradation (−68%) while π0.5 paradoxically improves (+11%), driven by a bimodal T9/T10 pattern where success depends entirely on spatial configuration.
Figure 9 Statistics of sub-task decompositions for each task.

Figure 6 Robot platform and evaluation environment. (a) Tabletop setup: bimanual 6-DOF follower arms inside the green-screen enclosure with fixed studio lighting (LED panel + softbox). A face camera captures the global workspace; wrist-mounted cameras on each arm provide close-up views. Master arms (foreground) are used for teleoperated data collection. (b) Mobile setup: Quanta X1 with mecanum-wheel omnidirectional base, height-adjustable lifting column, and bimanual ARX arms with wrist cameras, operating within a ∼3m × 3 m green-screen enclosure.

Figure 13 Per-trial score heatmap across 15 tasks. DreamZero’s pick_items row is uniformly green (9/10 perfect), while press_button is uniformly red. VLA models show higher variance—task-dependent patterns with sharp within-task fluctuations driven by spatial configuration sensitivity.
Figure 3 The overall statistics of ManipArena including the average trajectories of each task, the episode length, the sub-task count and other statistics.
